introduction:
The Ration Card in Uttar Pradesh is issued by the Food and Logistics Department (fcs.up.gov.in). As India's most populous state, UP has a massive PDS network covering both NFSA and Antyodaya beneficiaries.

The ration card is used to provide wheat, rice, sugar, and kerosene. In UP, the One Nation One Ration Card (ONORC) is fully integrated, allowing beneficiaries to lift ration from any Fair Price Shop across the state or country.
quickFacts:
UP PDS Info
  • Eligibility: Based on the SECC-2011 data and current income slabs.
  • E-KYC: Frequent biometric verification is required at the FPS.
  • Portability: You can change your FPS shop online easily.
documentsRequired:
Document Checklist
Requirements:
  • Aadhaar Card: Required for all members.
  • Bank Details: Copy of the bank passbook (Main female member).
  • Residence Proof: Electricity bill, Voter ID, or Sarpanch/Ward Member certificate.
  • Income Proof: Required for Patrata Grihasthi (PHH) cards.
  • Photo: Passport size photo of the Head of Family.
onlineApplication:
Online via CSC (Jan Seva Kendra)

  1. In UP, online application is primarily handled through Common Service Centres (CSC) or Jan Seva Kendra.
  2. Visit your nearest Jan Seva Kendra with original documents.
  3. The operator will fill out the application on the FCS UP Portal.
  4. The operator will scan and upload your documents.
  5. Pay the nominal service fee (approx. ₹30-₹50).
  6. Collect the application receipt with the ID number.
offlineApplication:
Offline via Supply Office

  1. Download the application form from the UP FCS Website.
  2. Fill the form and attach copies of Aadhaar and Bank passbook.
  3. Submit the form to the Tehsil Supply Officer (TSO) or Block Development Office (BDO).
  4. The department will process the manual form into the digital database.
afterApplying:
Verification & List Update

  • A field enquiry is conducted by the Lekhpal or Supply Inspector.
  • They verify your eligibility against the income criteria.
  • Once approved, your name will appear in the Eligibility List (Patrata Suchi) on the website.
